articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: Goal! FC Bayern München 1, Borussia Mönchengladbach 0. Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) header from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Michael Olise.
First Half ends, FC Bayern München 1, Borussia Mönchengladbach 0.
Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Joe Scally replaces Nico Elvedi.
Julian Weigl (Borussia Mönchengladbach) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Leroy Sané replaces Josip Stanisic because of an injury.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Aleksandar Pavlovic replaces Kingsley Coman.
Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Alassane Pléa replaces Philipp Sander.
Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Kevin Stöger replaces Robin Hack.
Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Tomás Cvancara replaces Franck Honorat.

Substitution, FC Bayern München. Serge Gnabry replaces Thomas Müller.
Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Florian Neuhaus replaces Rocco Reitz.

Goal! FC Bayern München 2, Borussia Mönchengladbach 0. Michael Olise (FC Bayern München) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Leroy Sané with a through ball.
Michael Olise (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Gabriel Vidovic replaces Harry Kane.
Second Half ends, FC Bayern München 2, Borussia Mönchengladbach 0.

article.headline: Champions Bayern beat Gladbach in Müller's final home game
article.description: Champions Bayern Munich eased past Borussia Monchengladbach 2-0 in the Bundesliga on Saturday as club icon Thomas Müller bid farewell in his final home game after 25 years at the club.
article.linkText: Champions Bayern win in Müller's final home game

article.story:
Champions Bayern Munich eased past Borussia Monchengladbach 2-0 in the Bundesliga on Saturday as club icon Thomas Müller bid farewell in his final home game after 25 years at the club.

The 35-year-old Müller, who came through the Bayern youth ranks to win dozens of titles including a record 13 league crowns and two Champions League trophies, was the focus even before the start.

He enjoyed a brief farewell ceremony with club bosses, complete with flowers and a picture of him and all the trophies, ahead of his 750th game for Bayern in all competitions.

But it was Gladbach who were in control at the start and in more of an attacking mood.

Bayern, who secured their 34th German league title last week, struck against the run of play when Bundesliga top scorer Harry Kane's header slightly deflected Michael Olise's shot in the 31st minute for the England captain's 25th league goal.

Kane is on track to become the first player in Bundesliga history to win the top scorer title in both of his first two campaigns, with one game left to play in the season.

Bayern eased up after the break and would have paid the price but for Manuel Neuer's successful comeback from injury with a string of saves. Olise made sure of the win in the 90th minute, shortly after Müller came off to a standing ovation.
